name: Additive Manufacturing
description: Advanced fabrication methods for metal, polymer, and composite components.
summary: Funding is concentrating around defense-relevant production and rapid prototyping pipelines.
caveats: Sample records are illustrative and not exhaustive.
Award coverage is not representative of all federal programs.

name: Biotech
description: Biological engineering, platforms, and translational therapeutics programs.
summary: Biotech awards remain broad, with sustained growth in platform technologies and translational pilots.
caveats: Dataset uses curated examples to demonstrate profile structure.

name: AI Safety
description: Methods for reliability, robustness, evaluation, and oversight of AI systems.
summary: Recent awards emphasize evaluation protocols and assurance methods for high-stakes systems.
caveats: Field boundaries are simplified for exploratory filtering.

name: Robotics
description: Autonomy, perception, manipulation, and deployment-oriented robotics systems.
summary: Institutional momentum is strongest where field robotics meets manufacturing and logistics.
caveats: Trend labels reflect local sample trend lines only.

name: Semiconductors
description: Materials, design, packaging, and manufacturing technologies for chips.
summary: Awards signal a strong push in domestic packaging and process innovation capabilities.
caveats: Agency and award mix is illustrative for prototype use.

name: Advanced Materials
description: Novel functional materials for energy, manufacturing, and healthcare systems.
summary: Cross-disciplinary materials research remains a connective layer across multiple funding programs.
caveats: Institution strengths are manually curated examples.

name: Energy Storage
description: Battery chemistry, grid-scale storage systems, and safety engineering.
summary: Momentum is strongest around resilient grid storage and domestic supply chain-linked materials.
caveats: Sample years and amounts are synthetic but plausible.

name: Digital Health
description: Clinical data, health platforms, and technology-enabled care delivery systems.
summary: Funding supports practical deployment pathways, especially for remote and equitable care models.
caveats: This prototype does not model patient outcomes.
